reasoning: Strong CBC padding oracle with three meaningful defense layers beyond the baseline: timing-only side channel (unified 400 responses, PBKDF2 10k rounds ~15-25ms delta over a 20ms base delay), position-dependent XOR padding scheme that defeats off-the-shelf tools, and a bijective IV rotation that breaks naive block-0 bit-flipping. The PoW requirement (3-zero-prefix SHA-256) adds realistic anti-automation friction that compounds with the statistical timing analysis needed per oracle query. Compared to previously validated 7/10 challenges with similar PBKDF2 timing oracles and custom padding, the IV rotation and PoW are genuine additional complexity layers that push this slightly higher — though the core attack remains a well-documented CBC padding oracle pattern.
